summary: Resend Email OTP Integration (FREE11) fully tested - Backend 100% (21/21), Frontend flow verified. RESEND_API_KEY loaded, Resend API called (403 domain-not-verified as expected), dev_otp correctly returned. 2-step Register UI flow works. One minor UX bug: wrong OTP shows 'Registration failed' instead of specific backend error 'Invalid OTP. X attempts remaining.'

ui_bugs.issue: When wrong OTP is entered, toast shows generic 'Registration failed' instead of specific backend error 'Invalid OTP. 4 attempts remaining.'. Backend correctly returns 400 with {'detail': 'Invalid OTP. 4 attempts remaining.'} (confirmed in network trace). The `if (!verifyRes.ok) { toast.error(verifyData.detail || ...) }` block should show the detail but shows catch block message instead.

action_items: MEDIUM: Investigate why wrong OTP shows 'Registration failed' toast (t('auth.register_failed')) instead of 'Invalid OTP. X attempts remaining.' (verifyData.detail). Backend returns correct error. Code path: Register.js line 83 `if (!verifyRes.ok) { toast.error(verifyData.detail || t('auth.otp_verify_failed')) }` - possible silent exception in toast.error() causing fallback to catch block, or closure/strict-mode issue.
INFO: Domain free11.com not verified in Resend dashboard - add DNS records at resend.com/domains to enable live email delivery
critical_code_review_comments: Register.js line 83: The toast error for wrong OTP falls through to catch block showing 'Registration failed' instead of the specific backend message. Suspected issue: the `if (!verifyRes.ok) { ... return; }` guard may not be catching the error path correctly. Consider adding explicit error logging: `console.error('OTP verify failed:', verifyData.detail)` to debug.

Register.js: No resend OTP button shown in the OTP step. Users cannot request a new OTP from the UI if they need to resend (e.g., after 60-second cooldown). Only `POST /api/auth/resend-verification` exists but no UI trigger in Register.js OTP step.

otp_engine.py: Resend rate limiting (429) - when running many concurrent users, 2 req/s limit from Resend will cause OTP delivery failures. The 429 fallback correctly returns dev_otp but in production this means emails not delivered. Consider queuing or rate limiting at application level.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (OTP flow works, 1 minor toast message bug)

test_credentials.note: Test emails used: TEST_otp_*@mail.example.com (do not register these)

seed_data_creation: OTP test records created in otp_codes collection for TEST_otp_*@mail.example.com emails. These are temporary and will expire/be cleaned up automatically (24h cleanup).

dev_otp is returned in API response as fallback when Resend fails
context_for_next_testing_agent: OTP email verification fully tested (iteration 29). Backend: 100% pass. Frontend: OTP flow works end-to-end. One MEDIUM bug: wrong OTP toast shows generic 'Registration failed' instead of specific backend error. To investigate: check Register.js handleSubmit error handling for OTP step (line 83). The RESEND_API_KEY is active and making real API calls to Resend but free11.com domain not verified (intentional for now). Previous iterations: 28=AdMob, 27=prior, next test should verify the toast bug fix if E1 fixes it.

wrong_otp_toast_generic_message.root_cause: Register.js line 83: `if (!verifyRes.ok) { toast.error(verifyData.detail || t('auth.otp_verify_failed')); setLoading(false); return; }` - the `return` statement SHOULD prevent the catch block. However, the toast shows 'Registration failed' (t('auth.register_failed')) which is only in the catch block. Possible causes: (1) silent exception in toast.error() or setLoading() causing catch to run, (2) React re-render causing double submission, (3) the fetch response.ok evaluation. Network trace CONFIRMS backend returns 400 with correct detail message.
wrong_otp_toast_generic_message.reproduction: 1. Fill Register form, click Verify Email → OTP step appears. 2. Enter wrong OTP (e.g. 000000). 3. Click Complete Sign Up. 4. Toast shows 'Registration failed' instead of 'Invalid OTP. 4 attempts remaining.'
wrong_otp_toast_generic_message.severity: MEDIUM - user gets wrong error message but OTP flow is not broken
